lessons.title: Alphabet Signs A-F
lessons.description: Master the hand shapes for letters A through F.
content: Keep your palm relaxed and fingers clearly visible.
Practice each letter slowly before increasing speed.
Maintain consistency in hand orientation.

lessons.title: Alphabet Signs G-Z
lessons.description: Complete the full alphabet with advanced hand placements.
content: Some letters require wrist rotation for clarity.
Use mirror practice to self-correct shapes.
Link letters together for finger-spelling fluency.

lessons.title: Numbers 0-5
content: Keep fingers fully extended where needed.
Avoid overlapping fingers to improve readability.
Repeat number transitions with rhythm.

lessons.title: Numbers 6-10
lessons.description: Continue number gestures with thumb combinations.
content: Focus on thumb-to-finger contact precision.
Hold each sign for one second before switching.
Practice in front of camera for confidence.

lessons.title: Greetings
lessons.description: Use common greeting signs in daily conversation.
content: Start with hello, good morning, and goodbye.
Pair signs with facial expression for tone.
Use smooth transitions between phrase signs.

lessons.title: Essential Phrases
lessons.description: Practice practical signs for everyday interactions.
content: Learn thank you, please, and excuse me.
Practice phrase speed while keeping form clean.
Review combinations in short dialogues.

lessons.title: Happy & Sad
lessons.description: Express basic emotions with accurate hand and face cues.
content: Facial expression is part of the sign meaning.
Keep sign movement gentle and intentional.
Practice contrast between opposite emotions.

lessons.title: Anger & Fear
lessons.description: Build expressive fluency with stronger emotional signs.
content: Use controlled movement even for intense emotions.
Anchor signs near consistent body positions.
Practice in sentence context for retention.

lessons.title: Starting a Conversation
content: Begin with greeting and self-introduction signs.
Maintain eye contact and clear pacing.
Use turn-taking cues with pauses.

lessons.title: Follow-up Questions
lessons.description: Keep dialogue flowing with simple follow-up patterns.
content: Use question expressions at sentence end.
Combine who, what, where signs naturally.
Practice short back-and-forth exchanges.

lessons.title: Speed & Clarity
lessons.description: Increase signing speed without losing precision.
content: Prioritize shape accuracy before speed drills.
Record yourself and identify unclear transitions.
Use interval practice for endurance.

lessons.title: Storytelling Flow
lessons.description: Apply advanced transitions in narrative signing.
content: Use spatial references consistently in a story.
Blend expressions, pace, and hand movement.
Review and refine your narrative coherence.

quizzes.question: Which principle improves sign readability most?
What helps most with finger-spelling fluency?
Which is an essential everyday sign phrase?
For intense emotions, movement should be:
